confusion: Confusing Robert Hooke with Robert Brown.
correctConcept: Robert Hooke discovered the CELL in 1665 (non-living cork). Robert Brown discovered the NUCLEUS inside the cell in 1831.
memoryTrick: Hooke hooked the Cell; Brown discovered the brain (Nucleus).

correctConcept: Mature mammalian Red Blood Cells (RBCs) lack a nucleus to maximize space for hemoglobin and oxygen transport. Exception: Camel and Llama RBCs have nuclei.
memoryTrick: RBCs lose their nucleus at maturity for oxygen space, except in desert camels.
